title: Switch Match
description: Match a single value against multiple possible cases using the powerful and exhaustive switch statement.
problemStatement:
Weather switch! Given a variable `weather` set to `"sunny"`, use a `switch` statement to print `"Wear sunglasses"` if it is sunny, `"Bring umbrella"` if it is rainy, and `"Enjoy the day"` for anything else.

**Requirement:** Include a `default` case to ensure exhaustiveness.
hints: Swift switch cases don't automatically fall through, so you don't need `break` statements between cases!
solutionCode:
let weather = "sunny"
switch weather {
case "sunny":
    print("Wear sunglasses")
case "rainy":
    print("Bring umbrella")
default:
    print("Enjoy the day")
}
